body:
Hey Sam,

Quick follow-up on what came up in standup this morning—Keanu wants the reef health metrics compiled by EOW for the funding report. I know we've got solid data from last week's dives, but the northern site is still giving us some processing headaches.

Can you grab the temperature logs from the dive computer and start organizing those by depth? Should take maybe an hour. Malia's swamped with the specimen photos, so if you can knock that out, we'll be in better shape for the data narrative.

Also, looks like conditions are favorable for Friday. Pool swim won't cut it—we should actually get back in the water.

—A

Hey Angela,

Already on it. Pulled the temp logs yesterday actually—had some downtime and figured it'd save us time. They're organized by depth in the shared drive under "2026-12-25_northSite_analysis." The gradient looks cleaner than the last survey, which is good news for the narrative.

I'll send you a quick summary once I finish the context notes. Malia mentioned the specimen photos should be ready by Wednesday, so timing works.

And yeah, Friday's a go for me. What time were you thinking? Water looks solid this week.

—S

Hi Sam,

Following up on yesterday's dive—the CTD data looks solid, though the thermocline was shallower than predicted at 18 meters. We logged three new coral colonies at the southern transect, so that's a clean addition to the database. Can you confirm you uploaded the video files from your camera? I noticed the underwater lens had some salt creep last time and want to make sure the footage quality held up.

Also, the institute pool is looking questionable for tomorrow's swim. Maintenance called about a potential pH issue. If it's still down, want to hit Sandy Beach instead? We can do the 6 AM slot if that works.

Thanks,
Angela

Angela,

Got it. Video files uploaded to the shared drive this morning—footage is clean, the lens seal actually held perfectly this time. I've also cross-referenced the thermocline depth with the NOAA buoy data from yesterday, and you're right, 18m is spot-on for the conditions we had.

Quick question: do you want me to flag those three new colonies in the database with preliminary health assessments, or should we wait until you review the photos? Also, I'll check on the pool status and let you know by tonight. 6 AM works for me either way.

Cheers,
Sam

Sam,

Good call on the lens. Let's hold off on health assessments until I review the imagery—I want to make sure we're not over-calling condition based on video distortion. I'll flag the colonies with 'pending review' status so they're noted but not locked in.

The pool situation is a mess, so Sandy Beach it is. See you tomorrow at 6.

—Angela

Hey Sam,

Quick question—I'm putting together slides for Keanu's check-in tomorrow, and I want to include the visibility and temperature data from last week's dive. Do you still have the raw logs from the eastern site, or did Malia grab those already? I remember the visibility was worse than predicted but wanted to confirm the exact numbers before I present.

Also, if you're around this week, we should probably grab coffee and debrief the conditions we observed. There's some algae pressure stuff I want to run by you before I bring it to the PI.

Thanks,
Angela

Angela,

Yeah, I've got the logs—I'll send them over in like 30 minutes. Visibility was 8 meters midday, dropped to 6 by afternoon. Temp was steady at 26.8°C. I also noted that surge pattern you flagged, so I included those observations too.

Coffee Thursday morning? The usual spot by the lab? I actually have some observations about substrate changes that might tie into your presentation.

Sam

Sam,

Got the logs, thanks. The temperature consistency is what I needed—Keanu's been asking about seasonal patterns, and this supports what we discussed about the thermocline shift timing.

Thursday works. Bring your notes on substrate changes. I'm trying to get ahead of questions about anthropogenic pressure, and that might be relevant for the next dive series we're planning.

See you then.
A

Perfect. I'll have the substrate notes printed out. Fair warning: they're not pretty, but they're what we saw. Thursday at 8?

One thing—after the meeting, let me know how Keanu reacts to the visibility trend. Might affect our dive scheduling for next month, and I want to make sure we're planning around optimal conditions.

Sam

Sam,

Quick follow-up on the pool session this Friday—did you confirm with the facility about the 6 AM slot? I want to make sure we're locked in before the institute books it for something else. I'm running behind on field data processing, so consistent swim time is keeping me sane right now.

Also, heads up that I might need to bail early if the dive logistics meeting runs long. Keanu's been pushing for a site assessment next week, and I need to get maps prepped.

Let me know what you hear back.

Angela

Angela,

Got the 6 AM confirmed—facility manager said we're good every Friday morning starting this week. They're actually grateful someone's using it that early; apparently the maintenance crew loves the empty pool time.

No pressure on the early exit thing. Just let me know when you're heading out and I'll wrap up whenever.

See you Friday.

Sam

Hi Sam,

Still hitting a wall with the Gmail API integration for the lab's dive schedule coordination. The service account authentication keeps rejecting the scope I'm trying to use for shared calendars. I need to pull event data from our group inbox so Malia can stop manually updating the spreadsheet—we're losing dates during rough swells when comms get spotty.

Did you have any luck with that documentation link you mentioned? I'm running this on our institutional server, so there might be permission issues on that end too.

Thanks,
Angela

Angela,

Yeah, I remember the pain. The shared calendar scope is finicky with service accounts. You need to make sure the service account email is explicitly added as a delegate to the shared calendar in Gmail first—that's usually what gets people.

Send me the error output and I can walk through the scope configuration. We might need to request a tighter scope depending on what you're actually trying to read.

Back in the water tomorrow, right? I'll bring my laptop to the pool.

—Sam

Sam,

Got it. The delegate access was the missing piece. It's pulling the events now, so we should be able to stop the manual updates by end of week. Malia's going to test the integration with the actual dive logs.

Thanks for the quick fix. See you at the pool tomorrow.

Angela

Sam,

Following up on the Google Calendar API integration. Got the OAuth credentials working, but the sync isn't pulling our dive site tags correctly. Calendar shows the events, but location data and dive codes are missing. Malia said you had a workaround for this—need it working before next week's field campaign so I can coordinate actual vs. planned dives with Keanu.

Doing this manually is a time sink. What do you need from me?

Angela

Angela,

The tag mapping issue—Google doesn't auto-extract custom fields, you have to explicitly set them in the request body. I have a Python snippet that handles this.

Two questions: events().insert() or batch update? And do you have service account auth already, or OAuth?

Can screenshare tomorrow morning to walk through it, or want me to just send the code?

Sam

Sam,

Ran the snippet you sent. Better, but now there are duplicate entries when it syncs. Something's firing twice—timing issue maybe? Also the location tags are showing wrong values. Says "Lab" for offshore dives, which defeats the purpose.

Can we sync up tomorrow morning before the lab standup? Need this locked down before the field campaign.

Angela

Angela,

Found it—the duplicate issue is the API call triggering twice in your cron. I've updated the auth credentials in the script to prevent that. For location mapping, verify your spreadsheet field names match the calendar schema exactly; even spaces break it.

Pushing the corrected version to the repo now. Should be solid for your dives.

Let me know if you hit any issues.

Sam

Hey Sam,

Following up on that Google Drive API issue from yesterday. We're still hitting the same auth errors when Malia tries to pull the reef survey data into our processing pipeline. I know you mentioned checking scopes, but we're getting 403s even after regenerating the credentials.

Can you take another look at the service account setup? I need this sorted before we can sync this week's field logs to the shared drive—Dr. Palani wants to review them by Friday. Let me know if you need the current credentials or error logs from my end.

Thanks,
Angela

Hi Angela,

Got it. I pulled up your setup and found the issue—the service account doesn't have editor rights on the shared folder. I've updated the permissions on my end, so it should be good now. Can you test it with a small batch of files first and let me know if the 403s are gone?

Also, while you're testing, let me know if the data sync is fast enough for your workflow. We might need to tweak the batch size depending on how many files you're pushing.

Talk soon,
Sam

Hey Sam—

Follow-up on the Zoom API thing we discussed last week. I need to get the lab standup recordings flowing into our shared drive automatically. Keanu wants historical data back to at least March for the repository.

Can you point me toward the right endpoint, or is this something you've handled before? Not urgent but the longer we wait the more standups we're manually archiving.

Let me know what you need from me to get started.

— Angela

Hey Angela—

Yeah, I can definitely help with this. The recordings endpoint should do what you need—you'll want to use the meeting recordings list endpoint with your meeting IDs. I've got a Python script that's been working well for batch pulls.

I can send it over tomorrow, but fair warning: their API rate limiting is brutal on the free tier. Let me know if you're still on that or if Keanu approved an upgrade.

— Sam

Perfect, send the script over when you get a chance. We're still on the free tier for now—Keanu's got it in the next budget round but that's a few months out. Honestly, even with rate limiting it beats manually downloading 12 weeks of recordings.

This would've come up sooner but we just got the storage sorted with IT. One thing: any gotchas with timezone conversion when these pull down? March stuff might matter since some of that was DST weirdness.

Thanks for this.

— A

Sam,

Just looped back on what Keanu said during standup about the monitoring protocol changes. Before we brief Malia on the new sampling intervals, I want to make sure we're on the same page about how this affects our dive schedule. Can we grab 15 min this week to walk through the revised timeline? I'm thinking Friday works, since we've got a solid forecast.

Also—did you catch the part about the funding timeline? Sounds like we might need to push the equipment order up by a month.

—Angela

Angela,

Friday works for me. I had the same concern about the sampling intervals—my notes show the new protocol basically double-checks our station spacing, which honestly might work better in the surge zones. I can prep a quick comparison doc before we talk.

On the funding side, yeah I caught that. Pretty tight timeline. We should probably sync with Malia sooner rather than later since she handles the purchase orders.

Talk Friday.
—Sam

Sam,

Perfect. I'll send you my notes on the surge zone adjustments—I was thinking the same thing about spacing.

Just sent Malia a heads-up about the timeline crunch. She's got the equipment vendors' lead times memorized, so at least we've got someone who knows where the bottlenecks are.

Attached a draft protocol comparison if you want to glance at it before Friday.

—Angela

Angela,

Good call on Malia—yeah, she'll know what's possible. Just reviewed your draft. The comparative stations look solid. One question on the data logging intervals: should we flag the transition stations separately, or keep them in the main dataset? Can discuss Friday but wanted to flag it now.

—Sam

Hey Sam,

Got the raw data logged from Tuesday's dive and the visibility was better than the forecast predicted—managed to get the full transect shots before the current picked up. The bleaching assessment spreadsheet is ready for Malia to cross-reference with the temperature logs.

One thing: the GoPro battery conked out on the third site. Might be worth grabbing fresh ones before the weekend dives if we're running back-to-back sessions. Also flagged a few GPS coordinates that need QA checks—nothing major, but Dr. Palani wants them locked down before we compile the report.

You around Thursday to prep gear and talk through the weekend plan?

—Angela

Angela,

Good call on the batteries—I grabbed a fresh batch yesterday, so we're set. I'll pull the GPS coordinates and run them through the system; usually those drift errors show up when we're in that kelp-heavy zone near site two.

Thursday works. How about 1 p.m. at the lab? I can bring the revised dive cards and we can map out the weekend sites with the new current data Malia pulled. Fair warning: the NW forecast is looking choppy for Sunday, so we might need a backup plan.

—Sam

Hey Sam,

Just wrapped the meeting with Keanu—went better than expected. He signed off on the Q3 timeline for the bleaching assessment, which means we can lock in our dive schedule. I need to get you the updated site coordinates and sample protocols by end of day tomorrow.

One thing came up: Keanu wants a preliminary analysis of the last two monitoring runs before our next check-in. I can handle the data wrangling, but I could use your notes from those dives—water temps, visibility, any anomalies you spotted. Do you have those logged?

Let me know when you're free to sync up.

Angela

Hey Angela,

Glad that went smooth. I've got everything from the last two dives—I'll compile the field notes and send them over by tomorrow morning. Visibility was rough on the second dive, but I logged all the temps and observations. The anomalies file is already backed up.

Also wanted to flag: I'll need to shift next week's dive schedule. I'm picking up extra shifts at the bar through Friday, but I'm clear after that if we need to do any prep work.

Talk soon.

Sam

Perfect, that timing works. I'll have the analysis ready by next Wednesday—that gives me enough time to cross-reference with Keanu's notes from last quarter.

One more thing: bring your underwater camera next dive if you can. Keanu's asking for better documentation of the bleaching patterns for the report. Nothing fancy, just the usual shots of the transects.

Thanks for pulling the logs so fast. Makes this whole thing actually doable.

Angela

Sam—confirming Thursday at 5 for the institute pool? I've got lanes 3 and 4 reserved. Fair warning: I need to keep it to an hour since I'm headed to the lab after to process the reef transect data from last weekend's dives. Still works for you? —A
Yep, 5 works. Just a heads up—the deep end pump's been acting weird, so we might get moved to the shallows. I'll check with the pool staff when I show up. See you Thursday. —S

Thanks for the intel. I emailed facilities and they said the pump should be fixed by Thursday, but we both know how reliable that is. Either way, we'll make it work. The water temp's supposed to be decent this week anyway. —A

Good—pump's apparently fixed now. Facilities just texted me back. Lanes 3 and 4 are ours. Looking forward to it. Could definitely use the mental reset before the dive briefing next week. Thursday at 5. —S
